title: 6 Enrolling in BPRP
body_text:
6	Enrolling in BPRP
6.1	Overview of Enrolling in BPRP
Enrolling a business member in BPRP includes the following steps:
Confirm the business member is set up as a Biller on Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Electronic Bill Payment System. See Section 6.3, Determining if the Business Member is Set up on Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Electronic Bill Payment System.
Complete the Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP), Form 2482, with the business member. See Section 6.4, Completing the Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing. A form must be completed for each Corporate Creditor Information Number (CCIN).
Complete the Corporate Creditor Agreement, Form 2697. See Section 6.5, Obtaining a Corporate Creditor Agreement.
Obtain a Letter of Direction ГўВҖВ“ Bill Payment Remittance Processing, Form 2533, from the business member. See Section 6.6, Obtaining a Letter of Direction ГўВҖВ“ Bill Payment Remittance Processing.
Send the Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP), Form 2482, and supporting documentation as described in Chapter 7, Sending BPRP Service Requests.
BC and Ontario Credit Unions and Other Central 1 Clients
Note:	Financial institution in other provinces, contact your provincial central for information on reporting and data file availability.
The following applies to BC and Ontario credit unions and other Central 1 clients:
Determine if the business member requires a data file. The data file allows Corporate Creditors to automatically credit their customersГўВҖВҷ accounts. The data file must be requested.
Provide File and Report Exchange procedures and file formats to Corporate Creditors. See Section 6.8, Providing File and Report Exchange Procedures and File Formats to Corporate Creditors.
Determine how the business member will connect to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange to obtain its reports and, if applicable, data files. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information.
Complete the FTP User Request, Form 2010, to grant the business member electronic access to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs transaction reports and, if applicable, data files. See Section 6.7, Accessing CentralВ 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange.
Note:	Central 1 is not obligated to provide BPRP for a financial institutionГўВҖВҷs business member.
Important
:	Even if a business member is enrolled in this service, other financial institutions can choose not to accept payments.
6.2	Enrollment Checklist for BPRP
BC and Ontario credit unions and other Central 1 clients use the Bill Payment Remittance Processing Checklist, Form 2661, to identify Corporate Creditor BPRP enrollment requirements. See Figure 1 and Figure 2.
Note:	Financial institution in other provinces, contact your provincial central for BPRP enrollment requirements.
Figure 1: Bill Payment Remittance Processing Checklist, Form 2661, Page 1
Figure 2: Bill Payment Remittance Processing Checklist, Form 2661, Page 2
Related Information
Bill Payment Remittance Processing Checklist,
Form 2661
6.3	Determining if the Business Member is Set up on Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Electronic Bill Payment System
To participate in Bill Payment Remittance Processing, the business member must be set up as a Biller on Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Electronic Bill Payments System.
To determine if the business member is currently a Biller on the Electronic Bill Payments System:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	YouГўВҖВҷll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itГўВҖВҷs your first login to the Client Centre, youГўВҖВҷve deleted your cookies, or youГўВҖВҷre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 3: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
.
See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
EBP Billers Listing
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Access EBP Billers Listing
Enter search criteria and then select
Search
. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: EBP Billers Listing
If the business member is a Biller on the Electronic Bill Payments System, note the Biller number. It must be recorded on the Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP), Form 2482.
If the business member is not set up on the Electronic Bill Payments System, Central 1 will enroll the business member as a Biller.
Note:	Contact Central 1, Client Support Services, for questions regarding Biller setup on the Electronic Bill Payment System. See Section 1.3, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
Related Information
Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP),
Form 2482
6.4	Completing the Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing
Complete the Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP), Form 2482, for each business member. See Figure 7, Figure 8, and Figure 9.
Completion of the form is self-explanatory, but note the following:
ГўВ‘
Indicate if the Biller is a Restricted Entity.
Figure 7: Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP), Form 2482, Page 1
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Ensure there is a biller contact for both implementation and tracing.
ГўВ‘Вў
Complete the Category Type, Account Validation Rules, and Electronic Bill Payment Remittance Processing Information sections. This information is used to facilitate setup of the service. In particular, in the Electronic Bill Payment Remittance Processing Information section:
Existing CCIN (if applicable)
ГўВҖВ“
The business member must have a CCIN to participate in BPRP. If this field is left blank, Central 1 will apply for a CCIN on behalf of the business member.
Central 1 Clients Only
:
ГўВҲВ’
Data Files
ГўВҖВ“
Indicate if the business member requires data files. If data files are required, check the box beside ГўВҖВңFlatГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңEDIГўВҖВқ to indicate the file type.
ГўВҲВ’
A data file allows the business member (Corporate Creditor) to automatically post payments to its clientsГўВҖВҷ accounts. Enter the internal system/third-party provider.
For more information, refer to Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ Flat File Format, Form 2665, and Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ EDI File Format, Form 2667.
Connectivity Option
ГўВҖВ“ Check the box beside ГўВҖВңClient CentreГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңSFTP Direct ConnectionГўВҖВқ to indicate the method the business member will use to connect to Central 1. The business member connects to CentralВ 1 to receive its transaction reports and, if applicable, data files. See Section 6.7, Accessing Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange, for more information.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Refer to this section for a list of documents that must be sent to Central 1 with the Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing. For example, some financial institutions can decline an application that does not include a copy of an invoice.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Select the financial institutions to send the billerГўВҖВҷs profile. Each financial institution may have its own enrollment process and agreements and may charge additional fees.
Figure 8: Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP), Form 2482, Page 2
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Ensure that the form is authorized by the business member, your financial institution, and the provincial central.
Figure 9: Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP), Form 2482, Page 3
Related Information
Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP),
Form 2482
Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ Flat File Specifications,
Form 2665
Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ EDI File Specifications,
Form 2667
6.5	Obtaining a Corporate Creditor Agreement
As per CPA Rule H6, your financial institution must obtain a Corporate Creditor Agreement, Form 2697, from each business member enrolling in BPRP. The requirements include:
A separate Corporate Creditor Agreement for each type of Remittance (e.g., utilities and property taxes), or a single Corporate Creditor Agreement for several types of Remittances, provided that each type of Remittance is associated with only one CCIN.
The Corporate Creditor (Payee) cannot enter into any other Corporate Creditor Agreement for remittances using the same CCIN while the existing agreement remains in force.
Submitting substantial amendments or termination of a Corporate Creditor Agreement in writing with a minimum of 60 daysГўВҖВҷ notice.
Your financial institution and its business member (Corporate Creditor) must comply with CPA Rule H6.
The information required on the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Corporate Creditor Agreement, Form 2697, Page 1
Obtain the Corporate Creditor Agreement, Form 2697, from the business member. Ensure that it is properly authorized.
Sign the agreement and return a copy to the business member.
Related Information
Application for Bill Payment Remittance Processing (BPRP),
Form 2482
Corporate Creditor Agreement,
Form 2697
6.6	Obtaining a Letter of Direction ГўВҖВ“ Bill Payment Remittance Processing
The Letter of Direction ГўВҖВ“ Bill Payment Remittance Processing, Form 2533, provides official notification to participating financial institutions that CentralВ 1 is the business memberГўВҖВҷs direct clearer for BPRP. The form is required for new Corporate Creditors and Corporate Creditors who move their BPRP to Central 1.
Note:	Letter of Direction ГўВҖВ“ Bill Payment Remittance Processing Cancellation, Form 9310, is used to terminate BPRP services. See Section 8.2, Cancelling a BPRP Arrangement.
Proceed as follows to obtain the Letter of Direction:
Provide the Letter of Direction ГўВҖВ“ Bill Payment Remittance Processing, Form 2533, to the business member. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Letter of Direction ГўВҖВ“ Bill Payment Remittance Processing, Form 2533
Advise the business member to:
ГўВҲВ’
Insert its logo on the document and enter the required information.
ГўВҲВ’
Complete Section A, New CCIN Biller, to set up a new CCIN
or
Complete Section B, Changes to CCIN Arrangement, to modify current CCIN arrangements.
ГўВҲВ’
Sign the letter and provide to your financial institution.
Important
:	You must instruct Corporate Creditors to review CPA Rule H6, Rules Pertaining to the Inter-financial Institution Exchange of Bill Payment Remittances for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement.
Related Information
Letter of Direction ГўВҖВ“ Bill Payment Remittance Processing,
Form 2533
6.7	Accessing Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange
This Section applies to BC and Ontario credit unions and other Central 1 clients. Financial institutions in other provinces, contact your provincial central for information on BPRP reports.
The business member (Corporate Creditor) connects to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange to download transaction reports and, if applicable, data files.
The Corporate Creditor requires access to the BILLPAY folder on the File and Report Exchange. The business member can connect to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on accessing the File and Report Exchange and setting up users.
6.8	Providing File and Report Exchange Procedures and File Formats to Corporate Creditors
This Section applies to BC and Ontario credit unions and other Central 1 clients. Financial institutions in other provinces, contact your provincial central for information on BPRP reports.
Provide the business member with the File and Report Exchange procedures:
ГўВҲВ’
Accessing the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre ГўВҖВ“ Business Member Procedures, Form 3430
ГўВҲВ’
Accessing the File and Report Exchange via Direct Connection (SFTP) ГўВҖВ“ Business Member Procedures, Form 3431
If the business member has requested an optional data file on their BPRP application, provide the business member with the file specifications:
ГўВҲВ’
Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ Flat File Specifications, Form 2665
ГўВҲВ’
Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ EDI File Specifications, Form 2667
Related Information
Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ Flat File Specifications,
Form 2665
Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ EDI File Specifications,
Form 2667
Accessing the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre ГўВҖВ“ Business Member Procedures,
Form 3430
Accessing the File and Report Exchange via Direct Connection (SFTP) ГўВҖВ“ Business Member Procedures,
Form 3431
END
